description: Write a Python script at /home/ga/SUMO_Output/analyze_centrality.py that bridges traffic simulation data with structural graph theory by computing the betweenness centrality of intersections in the Bologna Pasubio network (/home/ga/SUMO_Scenarios/bologna_pasubio/pasubio_buslanes.net.xml). Using sumolib and networkx, construct a directed graph of the road network where nodes are junction IDs and edges represent road connections, excluding all internal edges and junctions (those with IDs starting with ':' or marked as internal). The graph must use physical road length in meters as the edge weight. Compute the shortest-path betweenness centrality for all nodes using these weights. Export the full list of nodes and scores to /home/ga/SUMO_Output/node_centrality.csv with headers 'node_id,centrality', sorted by centrality in descending order. Additionally, save the IDs of the top 5 most central nodes to /home/ga/SUMO_Output/top_5_critical_nodes.txt (one ID per line). Execute the script to generate the required output files.
